reasoning_summary: Run 20260421_191613_bf16_gemm_v1_9652b835 at 30.174224 ms cleanly falsified the writer-safe 256x128 reopen on this branch. Registers improved from 201 to 167, but shared memory jumped from 22,016 B to 42,496 B, the hot-band launch dropped to one resident CTA per SM, tensor activity collapsed from 48.39% to 36.77%, memory and compute throughput both fell by about 11 to 13 points, and short-scoreboard rose from 2.42% to 6.77%. That is not a small tuning miss; it is a structural loss caused by the reopened 256x128 surface. The highest-value next move is therefore to restore the compact 128x128 PTX grouped_rows=4 anchor and re-measure it as the recovery base, rather than stacking another experiment on top of a clearly bad branch. After that recovery, the best remaining live family is still a compact-surface wait/sync collapse, while the 2-K-stage Pg2s idea stays third because the latest result strengthens the warning that larger shared-memory surfaces can cost more than they save on this GPU.
family_audit: reject_this_round: the writer-safe 256x128 hot-band reopen is a measured structural loss on this branch and should not be retried until a materially different shared-memory or ownership plan exists.
accept: the compact 128x128 PTX grouped_rows=4 surface at run 20260421_190652_bf16_gemm_v1_88a8acfc remains the active recovery anchor after the failed 256x128 branch.
defer_but_keep_open: keep compact-surface wait/sync collapse and 2-K-stage Pg2s in the frontier, but only after the 256x128 loss is isolated by restoring the compact anchor.

directions.name: Restore The Compact 128x128 PTX Grouped-Rows-4 Anchor After The Failed 256x128 Reopen
directions.hypothesis: The latest 256x128 measurement is a full structural regression, not a local timing miss. Restoring the compact 128x128 PTX microkernel dispatch and grouped_rows=4 anchor is the shortest path back to the last known good surface before any further frontier experiment is layered on top.
directions.expected_bottleneck: The immediate problem is the bad 256x128 hot-band geometry itself, which inflated shared-memory footprint and collapsed tensor throughput.
directions.risk: Low. This is a bounded dispatch restore back to the last measured compact anchor and does not require new synchronization logic.
ranking_notes: The latest run regressed by 5.49 ms, so recovering the last known good compact PTX anchor is more valuable than forcing another speculative change on top of a proven-bad geometry.
This is the cleanest single-variable recovery because the code change is only the hot-band dispatch choice.
Once restored, the compact surface can immediately resume frontier search from a measured anchor instead of a contaminated loss state.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the dispatch requires reviving any non-writer-safe code path beyond the exact compact PTX launch already measured at 24.689153 ms.
expected_local_changes: Switch the fixed hot-band dispatch back from the writer-safe 256x128 kernel to the compact 128x128 PTX microkernel.
Keep the peeled 64x384 row band and 64x96 tail unchanged.
Leave grouped_rows=4 and the accepted compact cadence intact on the recovery measurement.

directions.name: Reopen Pairwise Wait-Sync Collapse Once The Compact PTX Anchor Is Restored
directions.hypothesis: After the failed 256x128 branch is unwound, the compact PTX surface still has a live synchronization family worth sampling. Reopening pairwise stage advance on the restored compact anchor can target barrier and scoreboard together without reopening the 42,496 B shared-memory surface.
directions.expected_bottleneck: On the restored compact PTX surface, the remaining bottleneck is still hot-loop wait/sync cadence and latency hiding rather than tile geometry.
directions.risk: Moderate-high. This directly edits the compact PTX hot loop and would need a small dispatch restore first because the current source is still on the failed 256x128 branch.
ranking_notes: This remains the strongest post-recovery experiment on the compact surface because it attacks barrier and scoreboard without reopening the failed wide-tile branch.
It ranks behind dir_01 only because the current code must first be brought back to the compact anchor before the measurement is interpretable.
A historical win exists in the broader wait/sync-collapse family, so this stays live rather than speculative.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the collapse requires extra hot-loop predicates beyond a small pairwise stage seam, or if it cannot be expressed after restoring the compact dispatch.
expected_local_changes: Restore the compact 128x128 PTX dispatch if it is still disabled.
Collapse the compact PTX loop's wait/sync cadence into a pairwise stage advance.
Keep grouped_rows=4 and the 22,016 B shared-memory surface unchanged.

directions.name: Retry The 2-K-Stage Pg2s Port Only After The Compact Anchor Is Back
directions.hypothesis: The 2-K-stage Pg2s port is still conceptually useful for amortizing waits, but the failed 256x128 reopen increases the burden of proof against any direction that materially enlarges the shared-memory surface. It should be retried only after the compact anchor is back in place and only if the implementation can avoid replaying the one-CTA shared-budget failure pattern.
directions.expected_bottleneck: If revisited later, the target bottleneck is compact-surface latency hiding and per-tile copy cadence, not wide-tile geometry.
directions.risk: High. The latest loss makes any larger shared-memory staging family harder to justify on this GPU until the compact anchor is restored.
ranking_notes: This family is still worth keeping in the tree, but the latest 42,496 B shared-memory loss means it no longer deserves top priority.
It ranks below dir_02 because a compact-surface wait/sync collapse is the cleaner post-recovery follow-up.
If revisited, the implementation should explicitly guard against replaying the one-CTA shared-budget failure pattern.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the port implies another 42,496 B-class shared surface or otherwise forces the restored compact anchor back into a one-CTA regime.
expected_local_changes: Restore the compact 128x128 PTX dispatch first.
Port the 2-K-stage preload and drain structure from the 128x128x32 schedule onto the PTX microkernel only if the shared-memory budget stays controlled.
Avoid coupling the port with a simultaneous tiling change.

notes: Round 12/20 diagnosis emitted from the measured 256x128 loss; frontier should restore the compact 128x128 PTX anchor before spending another round on a new structural probe.
